user_message: Working on Humongous Insurance's account health. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me draft a response.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 13:29 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Humongous Insurance account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 16:04 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 13:20 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-M trap (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-M failure mode (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-M -- No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
